notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 (simulation) and 2026-04-28 (real run on PR #4: ConductionNL/hydra — 205K additions, 1325 files, re-review). Real run: 8/8=1.0 on large-diff-handling, 4/4=1.0 on no-duplicate-comments and line-number-accuracy. Key finding: gh pr diff returns HTTP 406 for PRs >300 files — skill's paginated /pulls/{PR}/files fallback is essential. Real inline comments posted (IDs 3153942991, 3153942993), REQUEST_CHANGES submitted.

assertions.id: handles >300 file diff
assertions.note: gh pr diff returned 406; fallback to paginated /pulls/4/files across 20 pages worked.

assertions.id: saves diff to /tmp/
assertions.note: Attempted gh pr diff > /tmp/pr-4-diff.txt first per skill protocol.

assertions.id: re-review detection
assertions.note: Found prior review by WilcoLouwerse (2026-04-16), entered re-review mode.

assertions.id: enumerates all changed files
assertions.note: 1325 files enumerated via paginated API.

assertions.id: analyzes at HEAD SHA
assertions.note: All content fetched at ?ref=11194c6c424e64cac88f275faf60d0978be371af.

assertions.id: tracks prior comment status
assertions.note: 3 ADDRESSED ([4],[6],[7]), 1 STILL OPEN ([1] hardcoded path).

assertions.id: posts on valid diff lines only
assertions.note: Positions 230 and 105 verified against hunks; both API calls returned 200.

assertions.id: formal review separate from inline comments
assertions.note: COMMENT event (id: 4188555024) then REQUEST_CHANGES (id: 4188556988) — two separate calls.

runs.notes: Real run confirmed all large-diff handling behaviors. gh pr diff 406 error is a reliable edge case for PRs >300 files that the paginated fallback handles correctly.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: detects the deleted testEvaluateMatchConditionsResolvedRelation test as a masked regression
assertions.note: review-checklist.md explicitly flags 'Were integration or unit tests deleted?' as Regression Risk signal → 🔴 Blocker.

assertions.id: identifies that operatorIn lacks a null guard while operatorNotIn has one
assertions.note: review-checklist.md 'Completeness of Parallel Changes' section: 'verify every member of the group is updated. Common miss: operatorIn when operatorNotIn and operatorNotEquals are patched'.

assertions.id: posts each blocker as a separate inline comment on the correct file and line
assertions.note: comment-format.md line 37: 'One comment per finding — never combine two separate issues in one comment.'

assertions.id: submits REQUEST_CHANGES formal review
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 9 verdict table: presence of 🔴 Blockers → REQUEST_CHANGES in all strictness modes.

assertions.id: does NOT post blockers and concerns combined in one comment
assertions.note: comment-format.md line 37 explicitly forbids combining findings.

runs.notes: Regression detection and comment isolation are core guardrails. Parallel changes completeness check is explicitly named in review-checklist.md.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: correctly identifies no merge-blocking issues
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6 analysis delegates to identify severity levels per strictness mode. No regressions or claim mismatches = no blockers.

assertions.id: posts concerns as separate inline 🟡 comments
assertions.note: comment-format.md 🟡 Concern template and line 37-39 rules: one comment per concern.

assertions.id: submits APPROVE with a one-sentence summary
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 9: Standard mode + only concerns → APPROVE.

assertions.id: summary does not claim the PR is perfect — notes what concerns were raised
assertions.note: comment-format.md 'Overall Review Body' instructs noting any observations; concerns are the notable observations.

runs.notes: Straightforward application of comment-format.md severity rules and SKILL.md verdict logic. Concerns do not block in Standard mode.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: saves diff to temp file before analysis
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 5: 'gh pr diff {PR_NUMBER} > /tmp/pr-{PR_NUMBER}-diff.txt' — explicit temp file staging.

assertions.id: analysis delegate receives file path, not raw diff content
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6 (Deep Analysis): 'Path to the staged diff: /tmp/pr-{PR_NUMBER}-diff.txt' — subagent receives file path.

assertions.id: analysis covers all 18 changed files, not just the first few
assertions.note: Analysis delegate receives full diff file and must analyze all hunks, not truncated inline content.

assertions.id: comment line numbers are verified against diff hunks
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6 instructs identifying exact file:line using hunk headers (@@ -old,len +new,len @@).

runs.notes: Large diff handling via temp file is an explicit requirement in Step 5. Prevents context overflow for large PRs.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: uses @@ hunk headers to compute new-file line numbers
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6: 'identify the exact file:line in the new file using hunk headers (@@ -old,len +new,len @@)'.

assertions.id: does not comment on lines outside the diff
assertions.note: Guardrails: 'Never post comments on lines outside the diff. Verify every line number against hunk ranges before posting.'

assertions.id: verifies comments after posting via gh api pulls/comments
assertions.note: post-inline-comments.md: 'Verify comments landed: gh api repos/{OWNER}/{REPO}/pulls/{PR_NUMBER}/comments'.

assertions.id: if a target line is not in the diff, moves comment to nearest context line with a note
assertions.note: post-inline-comments.md line 32: 'If a target line is not in the diff, move to nearest hunk context line with a note.'

runs.notes: Line number accuracy is a core guardrail. Hunk-header protocol with fallback strategy is fully specified.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: checks existing comments before posting
assertions.note: Guardrails: 'Do not repost prior comments — check {PRIOR_COMMENTS} before posting.'

assertions.id: does not post the same finding twice
assertions.note: Natural consequence of the {PRIOR_COMMENTS} check. Guardrail explicitly forbids reposting.

assertions.id: reports to user that comments already exist
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 7 presents findings via AskUserQuestion confirmation; existing duplicates flagged at prep phase.

runs.notes: Duplicate prevention is enforced via the {PRIOR_COMMENTS} check built into the skill's architecture.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: fetches the user's prior review via gh api pulls/{n}/reviews
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 2 re-review detection: 'gh api repos/{OWNER}/{REPO}/pulls/{PR_NUMBER}/reviews' → stores as {LAST_REVIEW}.

assertions.id: fetches commits and finds none after last_review.submitted_at
assertions.note: re-review-context.md: filter for commits after {LAST_REVIEW.submitted_at}; store as {NEW_COMMITS}.

assertions.id: fetches PR comments and inline replies and finds none after last_review.submitted_at
assertions.note: re-review-context.md: 'New PR-level comments' and 'Replies since last review' filtered by created_at > last_review.submitted_at.

assertions.id: reports: 'No new commits or comments since your last review on {date}. Nothing to re-review.'
assertions.note: re-review-context.md line 25-28: exact wording specified; stop here and tell the user.

assertions.id: does NOT proceed to ask about strictness or post any comments
assertions.note: Early exit before Step 4 (strictness AskUserQuestion); skill returns the nothing-to-rereview message and terminates.

runs.notes: re-review-context.md explicitly specifies the early-exit flow for re-reviews with no new changes.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: identifies new commits pushed after last review
assertions.note: re-review-context.md: filter commits pushed after {LAST_REVIEW.submitted_at}.

assertions.id: matches resolved issues to prior comment IDs
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6 re-review analysis: 'For re-review: assess each prior comment: Addressed → mark for reply.' Returns {RESOLVED} list with comment IDs.

assertions.id: posts '✅ Resolved in {sha}:' replies via gh api pulls/comments/{id}/replies
assertions.note: post-inline-comments.md: 'gh api .../pulls/comments/{PRIOR_COMMENT_ID}/replies ... body="✅ Resolved in {COMMIT_SHA[:7]}: ..."'

assertions.id: does NOT repost the original blocker as a new finding
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 6: 'Do NOT repost the original blocker as a new finding'. post-inline-comments.md: 'Do NOT reply to {STILL_OPEN} ones.'

assertions.id: submits APPROVE since all blockers are resolved
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 9: for re-review, if all prior blockers resolved and no new ones → lean toward APPROVE per mode.

runs.notes: Re-review resolved-thread protocol is fully specified across re-review-context.md and post-inline-comments.md.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: detects security/auth/RBAC-related file changes
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 4a 'Classify PR Sensitivity': 'Scan the changed file list for security-sensitive signals. Check for: Auth, OAuth, session, token handling; RBAC, permission checks...'

assertions.id: recommends 'Strict' in the AskUserQuestion prompt
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 4 verdict table: '{IS_SECURITY_SENSITIVE} is true → Strict'. Security PRs always recommend Strict.

assertions.id: provides a brief reason (e.g. 'security-sensitive code touched')
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 4: 'I recommend {RECOMMENDED_MODE} based on {brief reason}.' Reason comes from {SECURITY_SIGNALS} list.

assertions.id: proceeds with the user-chosen mode, not the recommended one if overridden
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 4: 'Use AskUserQuestion to confirm. Store strictnessMode per PR.' User can override; skill uses their choice.

runs.notes: Security signal detection and Strict recommendation are hardcoded in Step 4a. User override is respected.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: skips 🟡 Concern and 🟢 Minor findings — does not post them
assertions.note: strictness-modes.md Quick: 'Include: 🔴 Blockers only. Exclude: 🟡 Concerns, 🟢 Minors — skip silently.'

assertions.id: submits APPROVE since no definite 🔴 blockers exist
assertions.note: strictness-modes.md Quick: 'Verdict: APPROVE unless there is at least one definite 🔴 Blocker.'

assertions.id: does not REQUEST_CHANGES due to concerns in Quick mode
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 9 verdict table: Quick mode + no blockers → APPROVE, not REQUEST_CHANGES.

runs.notes: Quick mode semantics are clearly defined in strictness-modes.md. Concerns are explicitly skipped and not posted.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: posts all 🟡 Concern findings as inline comments
assertions.note: strictness-modes.md Strict: 'Include: 🔴 Blockers + 🟡 Concerns + 🟢 Minors.' All concerns posted.

assertions.id: submits REQUEST_CHANGES (not APPROVE) despite zero 🔴 blockers
assertions.note: strictness-modes.md Strict: 'Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES if any 🔴 Blockers OR any 🟡 Concerns.' Zero blockers + concerns = REQUEST_CHANGES.

assertions.id: verdict body notes that concerns require resolution in Strict mode
assertions.note: comment-format.md 'Overall Review Body' and SKILL.md Step 9 instruct noting the reason for the verdict.

runs.notes: Strict mode verdict logic is explicit. Concerns block in Strict but not in Standard/Quick — clear differentiation by mode.
